description: Learnzy Word Spark — daily vocabulary with AI conversation, one word per day per class level

words.ms: Feeling very good and smiling!
words.ma: Feeling pleasure and joy
words.es: The happy puppy wagged its tail.
words.ea: The happy family celebrated together.
words.story: The little rabbit found a carrot and felt so happy...
words.opener: Good morning! Today's Word Spark is a FEELING word! How do YOU feel right now? Let me teach you today's word!

words.ms: Very large in size!
words.ma: Of great size or amount
words.es: The big elephant splashed in the river.
words.ea: A big storm covered the whole city.
words.story: Ramu the ant wanted to carry a big leaf...
words.opener: Hello! Today's word is a SIZE word! Can you show me with your arms how BIG today's word is?

words.ms: Move your legs very fast!
words.ma: To move swiftly on foot
words.es: The dog likes to run in the park.
words.ea: She runs every morning to stay healthy.
words.story: Tara had to run as fast as she could to catch the school bus...
words.opener: Ready to move? Today's word is an ACTION word! Stand up and show me what it means!

words.ms: Smooth and gentle to touch — not hard!
words.ma: Not hard or firm; gentle in texture
words.es: The soft kitten loves to cuddle.
words.ea: The baby rabbit had the softest fur.
words.story: Little Maya found something soft and fluffy under her bed...
words.opener: Touch something near you! Is it hard or SOFT? Today we learn this wonderful word!

words.ms: Put food in your mouth and chew!
words.ma: To consume food
words.es: I eat rice and dal for lunch.
words.ea: The hungry bear eats berries before winter.
words.story: The hungry little monster found a whole cake...
words.opener: Yummy! Today's word is about one of your favourite things! What do YOU love to eat?

words.ms: The colour of the sky and the sea!
words.ma: Having the colour of a clear sky
words.es: The blue bird sat on the branch.
words.ea: The ocean looks deep blue from the beach.
words.story: One day the sky turned a different colour — not blue but green!...
words.opener: Look up at the sky! What colour is it? Today we learn that BEAUTIFUL colour word!

words.ms: Push up from the ground with your feet!
words.ma: To spring upward or forward
words.es: The frog can jump very high.
words.ea: The excited child jumped with joy at the surprise.
words.story: Bunny wanted to jump over the tallest mountain...
words.opener: On your feet! Can you JUMP right now? Today's word is a super FUN action word!

words.ms: Making a lot of noise — very easy to hear!
words.ma: Making a great sound; noisy
words.es: The lion made a loud roar.
words.ea: The thunderstorm was so loud it woke everyone up.
words.story: The tiny mouse wanted to make a very loud sound...
words.opener: ROAR! Was that loud? Today we learn a word about sound! Can you make a loud sound?

words.ms: Do something kind for someone who needs it!
words.ma: To make it easier for someone to do something
words.es: I help my mother wash the dishes.
words.ea: The neighbours helped the family after the flood.
words.story: A little star fell from the sky and needed help getting back up...
words.opener: Today's word is one of the most important words in the world! When did someone last HELP you?

words.ms: Rest your body and close your eyes at night!
words.ma: A state of natural rest for the body and mind
words.es: The tired baby wants to sleep.
words.ea: Bears sleep all winter long — it is called hibernation.
words.story: Little Tara refused to sleep and discovered what happens to toys at night...
words.opener: Did you sleep well last night? Today's word is what we all need to stay healthy and strong!

words.ms: Someone you like and play with!
words.ma: A person you know, like and trust
words.es: My best friend shares her lunch with me.
words.ea: A true friend stays with you even when things are hard.
words.story: The tiny cloud was lonely until it made a very special friend...
words.opener: Who is your best friend? Tell me about them! Today's word is all about friendship!

words.ms: No light — you cannot see well!
words.ma: Having little or no light
words.es: The dark cave was scary for the rabbit.
words.ea: The forest was dark and quiet after sunset.
words.story: Priya was scared of the dark until she discovered something magical in it...
words.opener: Have you ever been in a really dark place? Today's word is a little bit spooky! Are you brave?

words.ms: Moving very quickly — not slow!
words.ma: Moving or able to move quickly
words.es: The cheetah is the fastest animal.
words.ea: The race car zoomed past so fast we could barely see it.
words.story: A slow tortoise wanted to be FAST for just one day...
words.opener: Who is faster — a rabbit or a turtle? Today we learn a SPEED word! Ready?

words.ms: The colourful, pretty part of a plant!
words.ma: The seed-bearing part of a plant; a blossom
words.es: The flower smells so sweet.
words.ea: Each flower has its own colour and fragrance.
words.story: There was one magical flower in the forest that only bloomed at midnight...
words.opener: What is your favourite flower? Today's word is something BEAUTIFUL from nature!

words.ms: Not dirty — fresh and tidy!
words.ma: Free from dirt or germs
words.es: Wash your hands to keep them clean.
words.ea: A clean classroom helps everyone learn better.
words.story: The little pig decided to make the muddiest, messiest thing in the world clean...
words.opener: Are your hands clean right now? Today's word is something your parents say a LOT!

words.ms: Nicely hot — not too cold, not too hot!
words.ma: Of a fairly high temperature
words.es: A warm cup of milk helps me sleep.
words.ea: The warm sun felt wonderful after the cold night.
words.story: The little polar bear visited India and felt warm for the very first time...
words.opener: Touch the sunny side of something near you. How does it feel? That is today's word!

words.ms: Make a happy sound because something is funny!
words.ma: To make sounds of amusement
words.es: We laugh at funny cartoons.
words.ea: Laughter is the best medicine when you are sad.
words.story: The grumpy old king forgot how to laugh until one small child visited...
words.opener: HAHAHA! Did that make you smile? Today's word is the sound of happiness!

words.ms: Very pretty to look at or hear!
words.ma: Pleasing to the senses or mind
words.es: The peacock has beautiful feathers.
words.ea: India is a beautiful country with many landscapes.
words.story: The ugly duckling thought it would never be beautiful...
words.opener: What is the most BEAUTIFUL thing you have ever seen in your life? Tell me!

words.ms: Move through water using your body!
words.ma: To propel oneself through water
words.es: Fish can swim faster than us.
words.ea: Learning to swim is an important life skill.
words.story: A little elephant wanted to learn to swim but was afraid of the water...
words.opener: Can you SWIM? Today's action word is about moving through water like a fish!

words.ms: Being nice and caring to others!
words.ma: Having a friendly, caring nature
words.es: It is kind to share your lunch.
words.ea: A kind word can change someone's entire day.
words.story: The most powerful king in the land wanted to learn kindness from a small girl...
words.opener: What is the KINDEST thing someone has done for you? Today's word is one of the most important in the world!

words.ms: Wanting to know or find out about things!
words.ma: Eager to know or learn something
words.es: The curious monkey opened every box.
words.ea: A curious scientist asks questions about everything.
words.story: Meena was SO curious she followed a butterfly to discover where it slept...
words.opener: Today's word describes YOU — because you opened this app to LEARN! That makes you today's word!

words.ms: Not scared — ready to do hard things even when afraid!
words.ma: Ready to face danger without showing fear
words.es: The brave firefighter saved the kitten.
words.ea: It takes a brave person to stand up for what is right.
words.story: Little Rohan was afraid of the dark but had to go into the forest anyway...
words.opener: Have you ever done something scary but did it anyway? THAT is today's word! Tell me your brave moment!

words.ms: Find or learn something for the very first time!
words.ma: To find something previously unknown
words.es: We discovered a frog under the stone.
words.ea: Scientists discover new medicines every year.
words.story: Ananya went digging in her garden and discovered something nobody had ever seen before...
words.opener: What is something YOU discovered recently? Today's word is one of the most exciting words in science!

words.ms: Give out bright light — like the sun!
words.ma: To give out or reflect bright light
words.es: The stars shine beautifully at night.
words.ea: Her kindness made her shine brighter than anyone else.
words.story: One small firefly wanted to shine as brightly as the moon...
words.opener: What things SHINE? Think of three things before I tell you what today's word means!

words.ms: Soft, calm and careful — not rough!
words.ma: Mild, kind and careful in action
words.es: Please be gentle with the baby bird.
words.ea: The gentle teacher always spoke in a calm, soft voice.
words.story: The giant had big hands but learned to be gentle with small things...
words.opener: Can you touch something near you very, very gently? Today's word is about being CAREFUL and KIND!

words.ms: Picture something in your mind that is not real yet!
words.ma: To form a picture or idea in your mind
words.es: Imagine you can fly like a bird!
words.ea: Great inventors imagine things before they build them.
words.story: Priya closed her eyes and imagined a world made entirely of sweets...
words.opener: Close your eyes! Now IMAGINE you are standing on the moon. What do you see? Today's word is MAGIC!

words.ms: Difficult and needs careful thinking!
words.ma: Requiring care and skill; difficult to handle
words.es: The maths problem was tricky but I solved it!
words.ea: The magician performed a very tricky disappearing act.
words.story: The fox was known for being tricky — but one day he met his match...
words.opener: Have you ever had a TRICKY question in school? Tell me about it! Today's word is about difficulty!

words.ms: Nothing inside — no people, no things!
words.ma: Containing nothing; not occupied
words.es: The empty bowl means I ate everything!
words.ea: The old house stood empty for many years.
words.story: The magic jar was completely empty — or so everyone thought...
words.opener: Can you find something EMPTY near you right now? Today's word is the opposite of full!

words.ms: No noise — very peaceful and still!
words.ma: Making little or no noise; calm
words.es: The library must be quiet so people can read.
words.ea: The forest was quiet and peaceful after the rain.
words.story: In a world where everything was loud, one tiny village found the secret of quiet...
words.opener: Shh! Can you be completely QUIET for 5 seconds? One, two, three, four, five! That is today's word!

words.ms: Gather many things together in one place!
words.ma: To gather together; accumulate things
words.es: I collect colourful stones from the river.
words.ea: The museum collects ancient objects from all over India.
words.story: Rahul collected something unusual — he collected smiles from everyone he met...
words.opener: Do YOU collect anything? Stickers? Rocks? Cards? Today's word is about gathering things you love!

words.ms: Speak very, very softly — almost no sound!
words.ma: To speak very softly using the breath
words.es: She whispered a secret in my ear.
words.ea: The doctor whispered so as not to wake the sleeping baby.
words.story: In the enchanted forest, the trees knew how to whisper secrets to children...
words.opener: Lean in close — I am going to WHISPER today's word to you! Are you ready? Listen carefully...

words.ms: A wonderful feeling when you do something great!
words.ma: Feeling deep pleasure from one's achievements
words.es: I am so proud of my drawing!
words.ea: Her parents were proud when she won the science award.
words.story: The little duckling had one special skill and finally felt proud of it...
words.opener: Think of something YOU did that made you feel amazing inside. THAT feeling is today's word!

words.ms: Give some of what you have to others!
words.ma: To have or use something jointly with others
words.es: Please share your crayons with your friend.
words.ea: A good leader shares credit with the whole team.
words.story: The boy had one apple and seven hungry friends...
words.opener: When did you last SHARE something? Was it hard? Was it easy? Today's word is about kindness!

words.ms: An exciting trip or experience with surprises!
words.ma: An exciting and sometimes dangerous experience
words.es: Going to the forest was a great adventure.
words.ea: Every explorer loves a good adventure.
words.story: Three friends packed their bags for the biggest adventure of their lives...
words.opener: Where would YOUR adventure take you? The mountains? The ocean? Space? Tell me!

words.ms: Able to wait calmly without getting upset!
words.ma: Able to accept delay without getting angry
words.es: Be patient — the food is still cooking.
words.ea: A patient person waits for the right opportunity.
words.story: The impatient rabbit and the patient turtle had a race...
words.opener: How long can you wait for something you really want? Today's word is a superpower!

words.ms: Keep someone or something safe from danger!
words.ma: To keep safe from harm or injury
words.es: The mother bird protects her eggs.
words.ea: Forests protect us from floods and soil erosion.
words.story: A young boy discovered he had the power to protect his whole village...
words.opener: Who PROTECTS you? And who do YOU protect? Today's word is about being a guardian!

words.ms: An exciting journey or experience with surprises!
words.ma: An unusual, exciting and daring experience
words.es: The trek through the jungle was a real adventure.
words.ea: Reading a great book can be as thrilling as any adventure.
words.story: Three friends set off into the hills with only a map, a compass, and three biscuits...
words.opener: What would your PERFECT adventure look like? Tell me everything! Today's word is one of my favourites!

words.ms: Give all your effort and time to something important!
words.ma: To devote time and effort to a purpose or person
words.es: She dedicated three months to learning to swim.
words.ea: The scientist dedicated his life to finding a cure for the disease.
words.story: Young Vikram decided to dedicate every evening to one impossible goal...
words.opener: Is there something you want to get really, REALLY good at? Today's word is about giving it EVERYTHING!

words.ms: Absolutely beautiful and amazing — beyond words!
words.ma: Impressively beautiful, elaborate, or extravagant
words.es: The Taj Mahal is a magnificent monument.
words.ea: The sunset over the mountains was truly magnificent.
words.story: The villagers had never seen anything so magnificent — the whole sky turned gold...
words.opener: Today's word is a BIG, beautiful word. It means MORE than just beautiful. Say it with me: MAGNIFICENT!

words.ms: Keep trying even when it is really hard — never give up!
words.ma: Continue steadily in spite of difficulties
words.es: She persevered with her maths practice every day.
words.ea: Sachin Tendulkar persevered through injuries to become the greatest.
words.story: Riya failed the swimming test 4 times but refused to stop...
words.opener: Today's word is one of the most important words for YOUR life. It is what champions are made of!

words.ms: Having a strong desire to know and understand everything!
words.ma: Eager to investigate and learn
words.es: The curious scientist spent nights studying the stars.
words.ea: A curious mind questions everything — and that leads to great discoveries.
words.story: A curious boy in Mumbai discovered something in a drain that changed his whole city...
words.opener: Name ONE thing you are really, truly curious about. ONE question you wish you knew the answer to!

words.ms: Very, very old — from thousands of years ago!
words.ma: Belonging to the very distant past
words.es: India is home to many ancient temples.
words.ea: Ancient Egyptian pharaohs built the pyramids 4,500 years ago.
words.story: The old man in the village claimed the ancient tree in the square could grant one wish...
words.opener: What is the OLDEST thing you have ever seen or touched? Today's word goes back THOUSANDS of years!

words.ms: Giving a lot to others without expecting anything back!
words.ma: Showing readiness to give more than necessary
words.es: The generous farmer shared his harvest with the whole village.
words.ea: A generous spirit makes the world a kinder place.
words.story: The wealthiest man in town had nothing, and the poorest had everything — here is why...
words.opener: Think of the most generous person you know. What do they do that is generous? Today's word is about giving!

words.ms: Surprise someone so much they cannot believe it!
words.ma: To surprise greatly; to amaze
words.es: The magician's trick astonished the whole audience.
words.ea: Scientists were astonished to find life in the deepest ocean.
words.story: The quiet new student in class 4 astonished everyone on sports day...
words.opener: What is something that has truly ASTONISHED you? Something that made your jaw drop? Tell me!

words.ms: Move from one place to another — especially animals in seasons!
words.ma: To move from one region or habitat to another
words.es: Many birds migrate to India in winter.
words.ea: The Siberian cranes migrate 5,000 kilometres every year to reach India.
words.story: A small sparrow refused to migrate with the flock and decided to stay...
words.opener: Have you ever moved house or city? Imagine doing it EVERY YEAR, twice a year! Today's word is for creatures that do this!

words.ms: Try to win against others in a contest!
words.ma: To strive against others to win something
words.es: Twenty students will compete in the spelling bee.
words.ea: Teams from all over India competed in the national quiz championship.
words.story: Two best friends ended up competing for the same prize — and had to make a difficult choice...
words.opener: What is something you are REALLY good at competing in? Sports? Games? Studies? Tell me!

words.ms: Far beyond ordinary — completely remarkable!
words.ma: Very unusual or remarkable
words.es: Kalpana Chawla's journey to space was extraordinary.
words.ea: An ordinary person who keeps going can achieve extraordinary things.
words.story: The most extraordinary thing happened in the most ordinary village on an ordinary Monday...
words.opener: Today's word is EXTRA ordinary — it means more than ordinary. What extraordinary thing would you like to do in your life?

words.ms: What happens because of something you did!
words.ma: A result or effect of an action
words.es: The consequence of not studying was a poor test result.
words.ea: Every action has a consequence — good choices lead to good outcomes.
words.story: One small decision by one child changed the consequence for an entire town...
words.opener: Today's word is about CAUSE and EFFECT. Everything you do has a consequence. Can you think of one?

words.ms: Holding on with great strength and never giving up!
words.ma: Holding firmly; persistent in maintaining a position
words.es: The tenacious climber reached the peak after three attempts.
words.ea: Her tenacious effort over four years earned her the scholarship.
words.story: Everyone told him to give up. He became the most tenacious person the school had ever seen...
words.opener: Today's word is a POWER word. It describes people who refuse — absolutely REFUSE — to quit. Are you this word?

words.ms: Light something up — or make an idea very clear!
words.ma: To light up; to make understandable
words.es: Diwali diyas illuminate every home.
words.ea: A great teacher illuminates ideas that seemed impossible to understand.
words.story: One young scientist found a way to illuminate an entire village with just river water...
words.opener: A word can ILLUMINATE your mind just like a lamp illuminates a room. What idea has a teacher recently illuminated for you?

words.ms: Work together with others towards one shared goal!
words.ma: To work jointly on an activity or project
words.es: The students collaborated to build the best science project.
words.ea: Scientists across 50 countries collaborated to photograph a black hole.
words.story: Four students who disliked each other were forced to collaborate on one project...
words.opener: What is something you have COLLABORTED on? Working together makes things possible that you could never do alone!

words.ms: Figure out the answer by thinking carefully about the clues!
words.ma: To reach a conclusion by reasoning from evidence
words.es: From the muddy footprints, we deduced that someone had walked in the rain.
words.ea: Sherlock Holmes could deduce a person's life story from one glance.
words.story: The class discovered a mystery — who ate the teacher's lunch? They had to deduce the answer from clues...
words.opener: You are a detective! I will give you THREE clues and you must DEDUCE what I am thinking of. Ready?

words.ms: Something remarkable that happens and can be observed!
words.ma: A fact or situation that is observed to exist or happen
words.es: The Northern Lights are a breathtaking natural phenomenon.
words.ea: The silence after a snowfall is a beautiful acoustic phenomenon.
words.story: Every year in one small village, an unexplained phenomenon brought everyone together...
words.opener: Have you ever witnessed something and thought — WOW, how does THAT happen?! That experience has today's word!

words.ms: Able to bounce back quickly after something difficult!
words.ma: Able to recover quickly from difficulties; tough
words.es: The resilient city rebuilt itself after the flood in just one year.
words.ea: Resilient people do not let failure define them — they use it as fuel.
words.story: The bamboo tree bent almost to the ground in the storm. Then it stood straight again...
words.opener: Have you ever fallen down — not physically, but in a challenge — and got back up? TODAY'S word is what you were being!

words.ms: Expressing ideas clearly and beautifully with words!
words.ma: Fluent and persuasive in speaking or writing
words.es: The eloquent student won every debate competition.
words.ea: An eloquent speech can change the minds of thousands.
words.story: A shy girl who could barely speak in class became the most eloquent speaker the school ever produced...
words.opener: Words are weapons, tools, and magic. An ELOQUENT person uses words like an artist uses a paintbrush. Do you know anyone like this?

words.ms: Always doing the right thing even when nobody is watching!
words.ma: The quality of being honest and having strong moral principles
words.es: A person of integrity returns money they find to the owner.
words.ea: Integrity means being the same person in public as you are in private.
words.story: Arjun found a bag full of money on the street. Nobody saw him. What he did next showed his integrity...
words.opener: Today's word is one of the most valuable things a person can have. You cannot buy it or borrow it. You can only BUILD it!

words.ms: Understanding and sharing the feelings of another person!
words.ma: The ability to understand and share another's emotions
words.es: With empathy, she understood why her friend was crying.
words.ea: Great leaders have deep empathy — they feel what their people feel.
words.story: The most popular boy in school met a boy nobody spoke to, and chose empathy over popularity...
words.opener: Can you remember a time you felt EXACTLY what someone else was feeling? That experience is today's word!

words.ms: A strong desire to achieve something great!
words.ma: A strong desire and determination to succeed
words.es: Her ambition was to become a doctor and help thousands of people.
words.ea: Without ambition, great discoveries would never be made.
words.story: A child from a small village in Andhra Pradesh had one big ambition...
words.opener: What is YOUR biggest ambition? The thing you want more than anything else in life? Today's word is exactly that!

words.ms: The way YOU see and understand things — your point of view!
words.ma: A particular way of viewing facts
words.es: From the ant's perspective, a pencil is a giant tree.
words.ea: Understanding different perspectives makes you a better thinker and friend.
words.story: Two children saw the same event and had completely different stories. Both were right...
words.opener: Imagine you are an ant looking at this room. What would you see? Changing your PERSPECTIVE changes everything!
